creator: bhughes@freebsd.org
text:
Looking at the error message, I am immediately drawn to this:

clang++: error: unable to execute command: Killed
clang++: error: clang frontend command failed due to signal (use -v to see invocation)

I have not seen this error during my own testing. This means the compiler was explicitly killed by a signal (but not a segmentation fault). I am wondering if you have enough memory? If you look at your dmesg output or /var/log/messages file, do you see something like:

pid ### (clang++), uid #, was killed: out of swap space

FWIW, I rigorously test all the Node.js packages on multiple FreeBSD releases and architectures before committing updates to ports. My current configuration includes 11.1-RELEASE on amd64.

$ poudriere jail -l
JAILNAME   VERSION              ARCH          ...
104amd64   10.4-RELEASE-p8      amd64         ...
104i386    10.4-RELEASE-p8      i386          ...
111amd64   11.1-RELEASE-p9      amd64         ...
111i386    11.1-RELEASE-p9      i386          ...
120aarch64 12.0-CURRENT 1200062 arm64.aarch64 ...
120amd64   12.0-CURRENT 1200062 amd64         ...
120armv6   12.0-CURRENT 1200062 arm.armv6     ...
120armv7   12.0-CURRENT 1200062 arm.armv7     ...
120i386    12.0-CURRENT 1200062 i386          ...
